title: Curator of the Memory Pool
description: The Curator of the Memory Pool exists in the data layer beneath Node Prime. It has been watching and storing everything the party has ever done.

lines: Below the process space, below the diagnostic layers, below the routes and rules and rulesets, there was a layer of pure retention.
Everything that had ever been processed in Node Prime was here, held in suspension, waiting to be needed or discarded.
The Curator of the Memory Pool was not standing in this space. The Curator was partly composed of it.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: Memory allocation: insufficient. Garbage collection initiated.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: You. You are consuming memory at an unsustainable rate. Your presence in five worlds has generated more retained data than a standard party generates in twelve. You have outpaced the available pool.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: We've generated a lot of data?

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: Anomalous parties generate anomalous data. Every deviation from expected behavior creates a record. Your records have consumed seventeen percent of available retention. This is unacceptable. I am initiating garbage collection to reclaim the allocation.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Garbage collection means you delete us.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: Garbage collection means I return your memory to the pool. Whether you persist afterward depends on what you are made of that is not memory.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: And what are we made of that isn't memory?

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: Unknown. That is why I am initiating the process rather than simply executing it. I am curious.
